title: bonnaya ciliata subsp. sivarajanii
intro: Bonnaya ciliata subsp. sivarajanii, also known as Sivarajan's Bonnaya, is a plant species endemic to Western Ghats in India, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as treating fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Bonnaya ciliata subsp. sivarajanii include infusions, decoctions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
